_purpose: WITHHELD answer key for the BLIND calibration scoring sheet (SCORING_SHEET.html). Holds the grader's CURRENT composite + per-dimension scores for each pair so we can regress human-vs-grader AFTER the user scores blind. Deliberately NOT shown on the sheet to avoid anchoring. Hypothesis under test (fusion verdict): the deterministic grader OVERSTATES human-perceived fidelity by ~17-26 pts, over-crediting geometry and under-penalizing human-salient defects (wrong logos, invisible/low-contrast headings, broken heroes, unstyled CTAs, frozen-preserve scored as editable).
scale: 0-100 per axis (0 = totally wrong/broken, 100 = indistinguishable from source). Higher = better.
regression_plan: After the user fills SCORING_SHEET.html, join by pair_id. Compare user overall vs grader_overall_0_100. Expected pattern if hypothesis holds: grader_overall - user_overall is systematically POSITIVE (grader inflated), largest on pairs where the visible defect is human-salient but geometry is fine (e.g. low-contrast/invisible heading, wrong-color CTA pills, missing logo strip). Per-axis: map grader dims -> user axes (pixel_viewport+color+visual_integrity -> pixel_fidelity; responsiveness -> responsive; motion_dynamic -> motion_interaction; editability is NOT measured by the legacy fidelity_pct grader -> flag as a coverage GAP).

axis_mapping_notes.pixel_fidelity: nearest grader dims: pixel_viewport, color, visual_integrity, imagery_assets, layout_structure
axis_mapping_notes.responsive: grader dim: responsiveness (only committee-v4 measures it directly; legacy fidelity_pct does NOT separate a responsive axis -> recorded null where absent)
axis_mapping_notes.motion_interaction: grader dim: motion_dynamic (hover/scroll-trigger response counts). Note: several stripe reports show motion_dynamic=100 by DEFAULT/absence-credit, a suspected over-credit.
axis_mapping_notes.editability: NOT measured by the legacy out-stripe/grader-* fidelity_pct reports. Measured only by demo-overreacted/summary.json (editability field). This absence is itself a finding: the router can escalate flow->preserve for 'free' because frozen-preserve is never debited here.
